title: Casey Tanaka — GTM Advisor
compiled_truth:
Casey Tanaka is a go-to-market advisor at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), the deterministic data infrastructure company building single-pass primitives for compression, authentication, and transmission. Casey's involvement predates the formal advisory agreement — the founding team had been passing around a draft of Casey's internal sales playbook from a previous infrastructure company months before anyone thought to reach out directly.

Before Anchor, Tanaka spent roughly fourteen years in enterprise sales leadership across three infrastructure startups, two of which exited via acquisition and one that went public in a modest but respectable IPO. The throughline across all three was the same: Casey figured out how to sell deeply technical products to buyers who didn't want to understand the internals. That's the skill. Not the pitch deck, not the pricing model — though Casey has strong opinions on both — but the narrative framing that makes a CTO feel like they already understand why they need the thing.

People describe Casey as a storyteller first and a sales leader second. There's a recurring bit in internal Anchor lore about how Casey once closed a seven-figure deal by drawing a single diagram on a napkin and talking for forty minutes straight. Whether that's apocryphal or not, it captures something real about how Tanaka operates. Distribution-focused to the core. Casey's default question in any product conversation is "who's going to buy this and what do they call their problem?"

The GTM playbook [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) is currently executing — the one that segments prospects by data volume tier and leads with compliance narratives before introducing performance gains — is essentially Casey's framework adapted for Anchor's market. Tanaka reviews pipeline weekly with the sales leads, though the formal advisory commitment is only about 8 hours per month. In practice it's more like 12-15, because Casey tends to get pulled into deal reviews and occasionaly joins customer calls when the stakes are high enough.

Casey is based in Seattle, prefers async communication, and is notorious for sending voice memos instead of typing. Known to be direct to the point of bluntness, especially when reviewing messaging drafts. The team respects it even when it stings.
timeline:
- **2026-03-12** | Casey Tanaka's GTM playbook from a prior infrastructure company begins circulating internally at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), shared by the founding team.
- **2026-06-01** | Formal advisory agreement signed between Casey and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor); initial scope set at 8 hours/month.
- **2026-09-18** | Casey leads a full-day GTM workshop with Anchor's early sales hires, introducing the tiered prospecting framework.
- **2027-01-10** | Tanaka joins a critical enterprise deal call with a Fortune 500 prospect; deal closes three weeks later.
- **2027-04-22** | Casey presents "Selling Primitives to People Who Don't Know They Need Them" at an internal all-hands, later adapted into an external conference talk.
- **2027-07-08** | Advisory scope informally expands; Casey begins attending weekly pipeline reviews alongside the sales leads.
- **2027-11-03** | Tanaka flags misalignment between Anchor's marketing messaging and actual buyer language; triggers a messaging overhaul that takes about six weeks.
- **2028-02-14** | Casey helps structure [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)'s first channel partner program, drawing on playbooks from previous infrastructure roles.
- **2028-06-20** | Gives a keynote at a regional infrastructure conference in Portland on distribution strategies for deeply technical products.

_facts.name: Casey Tanaka
_facts.title: GTM Advisor
_facts.background: Enterprise sales leader at three infrastructure companies; wrote the GTM playbook Anchor is executing
